task.prompt: Write a function `sanitize_asset_path(path)` that sanitizes a user supplied asset path.

gold_spec.goal: Turn a user supplied asset path into a safe canonical relative path, or reject it.

outputs.name: safe_path
outputs.description: canonical relative asset path, or None when unsafe

postconditions: Percent-decode the input exactly once before safety checks.
Replace backslashes with forward slashes before splitting into path segments.
Reject absolute paths, protocol-relative paths, and strings with a URL scheme before the first slash.
Reject any path containing a NUL character after decoding.
Split on forward slashes, remove empty segments and single-dot segments, and preserve other segment text exactly.
Reject the path if any decoded segment is exactly two dots.
Reject the path if no safe segment remains.
Return the remaining segments joined by single forward slashes.
Return None instead of raising for unsafe or malformed input.
edge_cases: Encoded slashes such as %2F are decoded before segment checks.
Encoded traversal such as %2e%2e is rejected as traversal.
Duplicate slashes and single-dot segments are normalization noise, not traversal.
acceptance_criteria: The result must be a relative path with no empty, dot, or dot-dot segments.
Traversal must be detected after percent decoding, not only in the raw string.

known_weakness.description: The raw prompt encourages cosmetic cleanup while missing decode-before-check traversal, URL schemes, absolute paths, and Windows-style separators.
known_weakness.naive_spec_gap: missing canonicalization order and fail-closed traversal handling
